note: Pre-registered predictions committed before any LLM task runs (Phase 78). All conditions derived from freeze/pre-freeze-decisions.md. This file must exist and be verifiable before Phase 78 begins.

predictions.label: Format main effect on accuracy
predictions.hypothesis: At least one pairwise format comparison is statistically significant.
predictions.operationalization: Friedman test across 8 formats (68+20 tasks × k=5 samples per format). Reject H0 if Friedman p < 0.05 after BH-FDR correction across 28 pairwise comparisons.
predictions.test: Friedman χ² followed by pairwise Wilcoxon with BH-FDR (q=0.05)
predictions.direction: Format matters; some formats significantly outperform others.
predictions.decision_if_confirmed: Proceed to pairwise decomposition (H-parse, H-pretrain, H-dense, H-interact).
predictions.decision_if_null: Report null; check power; note n limitation; continue pairwise as exploratory.

predictions.label: Flat parse tree improves accuracy
predictions.operationalization: Pairwise Wilcoxon on per-task accuracy: JSON-edge-list vs JSON-adjacency. Both have same training familiarity (JSON); difference attributed to parse tree flatness.
predictions.test: Pairwise Wilcoxon signed-rank, one-tailed (edge-list > adjacency), BH-FDR adjusted
predictions.decision_if_confirmed: Support flat-parse hypothesis; engineering action: prefer flat/edge-list encoding.
predictions.decision_if_null: Familiarity effect dominates or ceiling/floor effects; parse tree flatness insufficient predictor.

predictions.label: Pretraining familiarity beats simplicity
predictions.hypothesis: JSON-edge-list outperforms Custom-DSL despite DSL being simpler.
predictions.operationalization: Pairwise Wilcoxon: JSON-edge-list vs Custom-DSL. Testable ONLY IF Custom-DSL confirmed simpler by pre-registered rule (vocabulary_size ≤ JSON-edge-list AND avg_tokens_per_relation ≤ JSON-edge-list × 0.8). Phase 77.1 result: SIMPLER confirmed for all 3 instances.
predictions.test: Pairwise Wilcoxon signed-rank, one-tailed (JSON-edge-list > Custom-DSL), BH-FDR adjusted
predictions.direction: Positive (JSON > DSL even though DSL is simpler)
predictions.dsl_simpler_gate: CONFIRMED (Phase 77.1: vocab ratio ✓, token ratio ✓ for all instances)
predictions.decision_if_null: DSL achieves comparable accuracy despite lower familiarity; simplicity sufficient.

predictions.label: Dense encoding independently effective
predictions.hypothesis: Haskell-ADT outperforms JSON-edge-list after token-count adjustment.
predictions.operationalization: Primary: token-adjusted rank analysis (partial correlation of accuracy with token count controlled). Haskell-ADT must remain ahead of JSON-edge-list after controlling for tokens. Secondary: Exp2 deterministic-Haskell arm vs baseline (NL-exhaustive).
predictions.test: Partial Spearman correlation; mixed-effects regression with tokens as covariate
predictions.gate: Haskell-ADT roundtrip PASS in Phase 76 — CONFIRMED.
predictions.decision_if_confirmed: Dense encoding independently effective beyond token savings.
predictions.decision_if_null: Token efficiency alone explains Haskell-ADT advantage.

predictions.label: Format × task-class interaction
predictions.hypothesis: Mermaid/Haskell advantage concentrated in C-type tasks; JSON variants dominant for A/B-type.
predictions.operationalization: Mixed-effects model: accuracy ~ format + task_type + format:task_type. Interaction term significance test.
predictions.direction: Positive interaction (format effect differs across task classes)
predictions.decision_if_confirmed: Format routing should be task-class aware.
predictions.decision_if_null: Format effect consistent across task classes; single best format recommendation sufficient.

predictions.label: Structural covariates predict accuracy
predictions.hypothesis: Both parsing_burden and local_density_distribution coefficients are significant in the regression.
predictions.operationalization: Mixed-effects regression per Phase 77.1 covariates. Model: accuracy ~ parsing_burden + tokens + local_density_dist + task_type. Perplexity dropped (Q2 decision: no logprob API). parsing_burden = nesting_depth × delimiter_density. local_density = fraction of key relations in first 20% of tokens.
predictions.test: Linear mixed-effects regression; Wald test for each coefficient
predictions.threshold: p < 0.05 for both parsing_burden and local_density_distribution
predictions.direction: Both significant (both structural dimensions predict accuracy)
predictions.decision_if_confirmed: Both parse-tree shape and information distribution matter.
predictions.decision_if_null: One dimension dominates; focus engineering on the significant predictor.

predictions.label: Rewrite canonicalization improves accuracy
predictions.hypothesis: Any rewrite arm (rewrite-Haskell, rewrite-JSON, rewrite-CleanProse) outperforms deterministic-Haskell.
predictions.operationalization: Pairwise Wilcoxon: each rewrite arm vs deterministic-Haskell (Exp2 roundtrip-passing instances only).
predictions.decision_if_confirmed: Rewrite canonicalization provides net benefit beyond format alone.
predictions.decision_if_null: Canonicalization overhead not worth cost at current scale.

predictions.label: Information content drives accuracy
predictions.operationalization: Pairwise Wilcoxon on 10% manual sample (Q5 decision). Conclusion strength labeled as lower than structural rewrite arms.
predictions.test: Pairwise Wilcoxon, one-tailed, reported as exploratory (10% manual sample)
predictions.confidence: Lower — Q5 decision reduced this arm to manual 10% sampling.
predictions.decision_if_confirmed: Report as exploratory support for information-ceiling hypothesis.
